questions.question_text: Which point lies on the line $y = 2x + 1$?

options.why: Subtracted the constant instead of adding: $2(3) - 1 = 5$.

questions.question_text: Which point does **not** lie on the line $y = x + 4$?

options.why: $1 + 4 = 5$, so $(1, 5)$ does lie on the line.
$3 + 4 = 7$, so $(3, 7)$ does lie on the line.
$0 + 4 = 4$, so $(0, 4)$ does lie on the line.

questions.solution_text:
$x + 4$
$= (2) + 4$
$= 6$
Since $6 \ne 7$, $(2, 7)$ does not lie on the line.

options.why: $B$ sits above the line; at $x = 1$ the line gives $y = 1$.
$D$ sits below the line; at $x = 3$ the line gives $y = 5$.
$A$ sits above the line; at $x = 0$ the line gives $y = -1$.

questions.solution_text:
$2x - 1$
$= 2(2) - 1$
$= 3$
So $C = (2, 3)$ lies on the line; the other points do not.

questions.question_text: The point $(3, k)$ lies on the line $y = 2x + 1$. What is the value of $k$?

options.why: Subtracted the constant instead of adding: $2(3) - 1 = 5$.

questions.question_text: The point $(-2, k)$ lies on the line $y = -3x + 1$. What is the value of $k$?
